pi_items.source: Task specification in task.json description - agent creates this test aircraft record

Task specification in task.json description - agent creates this test registration mark. Format is plausible for Indian drone registration (IND=India, UP=Uttar Pradesh, 2024=year, 003=sequential).

Task specification in task.json description - agent selects/creates this test company name. Web search found no existing company by this exact name registered as a drone operator.
Task specification in task.json description - agent creates this test pilot record. Web search found no existing person by this name known as a professional pilot.

Task specification in task.json description - agent creates this test manufacturer record. Note: Aerobridge is a software system for drone fleet management, not a drone manufacturer. This is a test/fictional manufacturer name.
Verified in setup_task.sh (line 74) and README.md (line 22) - Django admin interface of local Aerobridge instance

Verified in task.json description and README.md - hardcoded test password for local environment

privileged_info_summary: Aerobridge is verified as an open-source drone fleet management system (Django-based). The task executes in a local development environment with Django admin credentials (admin/adminpass123) at http://localhost:8000/admin/. All metadata values are test specifications: the aircraft 'Falcon Eye 3', flight controller ID 'FE3CTRL334455', and registration mark 'IND/UP/2024/003' are synthetic test values that the agent creates during task execution. The operator 'A.J. August Photography' and pilot 'Rakesh Kankipati' are test identities not found in public records. The registration mark format (IND/UP/2024/003) is plausible for Indian drone registration standards.
